name: Interaction Layer
description: Configure XR interaction systems, locomotion, and informational heritage interactions inside the Unity XR scene.

steps.title: Configure XR Origin and OpenXR Interaction Systems
steps.description: Prepare the core XR player rig, controller tracking, and locomotion systems required for VR scene interaction.
instructions: Ensure the OpenXR plugin and XR Interaction Toolkit are already installed and enabled in the Unity project.
Inside Unity, add an XR Origin prefab to the scene using the XR Interaction Toolkit samples or prefabs.
Verify that the XR Origin contains a Main Camera and tracked controller objects for left and right hands.
Configure locomotion systems such as teleportation or continuous movement depending on the intended user experience.
For beginner-friendly VR experiences, teleportation is generally recommended to reduce motion sickness.
Add teleportation areas or teleport anchors onto walkable surfaces within the monument environment.
Verify that controller tracking and headset movement function correctly inside Play mode.
Test the XR rig using the target hardware or XR simulator before continuing scene development.
checklist: XR tracking verified in Play mode

steps.title: Create Monument Interaction Hotspots
steps.description: Add informational interaction points to important monument features and heritage objects.
instructions: Identify key architectural, historical, or cultural points of interest within the monument scene.
Create hotspot objects using colliders, UI canvases, or reusable interaction prefabs.
Place hotspots near carvings, statues, inscriptions, entrances, or historically significant structures.
Author descriptive information for each hotspot including historical context, architectural details, or cultural relevance.
Link hotspot triggers to information panels, audio narration, or visual overlays where appropriate.
Ensure hotspots remain accessible and readable within VR without obstructing navigation.
Test hotspot activation distance and interaction responsiveness using XR controllers.

steps.title: Validate XR Interactions and Scene Performance
steps.description: Test the VR scene to ensure interactions, locomotion, and rendering performance function correctly on XR hardware.
instructions: Enter Play mode in Unity and test the scene using XR hardware or the XR simulator.
Verify that headset tracking, controller input, and locomotion systems operate correctly.
Walk through the scene and trigger each hotspot interaction to confirm proper functionality.
Inspect the Unity console for missing references, script errors, or interaction warnings.
Monitor scene performance and ensure the frame rate remains stable for comfortable VR usage.
Reduce unnecessary lighting complexity, excessive polygons, or oversized textures if performance issues occur.
Verify that monument scale, interaction distances, and movement speeds feel natural inside VR.
